subject: Biology
title: Tropisms: Growing Toward Signals
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Tropisms: Growing Toward Signals.

hook.text: Plants can't walk toward sunlight, but they can grow toward it. This bending is called tropism.

buildup.text: Phototropism bends stems toward light. Gravitropism sends roots downward and shoots upward.

discovery.text: Thigmotropism lets vines wrap around supports when they touch a surface.

twist.text: In space, without gravity, roots grow in random directions—confused without their usual signal.

climax.text: Tropisms show that plants sense and respond to their environment in real time.

punchline.text: Plants can't walk. They grow their way there.
